heading: Strategic plan for sustainable chemistry
label: 9302 Strategic plan for sustainable chemistry
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsecs. children.text: consult with relevant stakeholders, including representatives from industry, academia, national labs, the Federal Government, and international entities, to develop and update, as needed, a consensus definition of “sustainable chemistry” to guide the activities under this chapter;
develop a working framework of attributes characterizing, and metrics for assessing, sustainable chemistry, as described in subsection (b);

assess the state of sustainable chemistry in the United States as a key benchmark from which progress under the activities described in this chapter can be measured, including assessing key sectors of the United States economy, key technology platforms, commercial priorities, and barriers to innovation;

coordinate and support Federal research, development, demonstration, technology transfer, commercialization, education, and training efforts in sustainable chemistry, including budget coordination and support for public-private partnerships, as appropriate;

identify any Federal regulatory barriers to, and opportunities for, Federal agencies facilitating the development of incentives for development, consideration, and use of sustainable chemistry processes and products;
identify major scientific challenges, roadblocks, and hurdles to transformational progress in improving the sustainability of the chemical sciences; and
review, identify, and make effort to eliminate duplicative Federal funding and duplicative Federal research in sustainable chemistry.

children.heading: Characterizing and assessing sustainable chemistry
children.label: b Characterizing and assessing sustainable chemistry

children.text: seek advice and input from stakeholders as described in subsection (c);
consider existing definitions of, or frameworks characterizing and metrics for assessing, sustainable chemistry already in use at Federal agencies;

consider existing definitions of, or frameworks characterizing and metrics for assessing, sustainable chemistry already in use by international organizations of which the United States is a member, such as the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development; and
consider any other appropriate existing definitions of, or frameworks characterizing and metrics for assessing, sustainable chemistry.
business and industry, including trade associations and small- and medium-sized enterprises from across the value chain;
the scientific community, including the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine, scientific professional societies, national labs, and academia;
the defense community;
State, tribal, and local governments, including nonregulatory State or regional sustainable chemistry programs, as appropriate;
nongovernmental organizations; and
other appropriate organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: a summary of federally funded sustainable chemistry research, development, demonstration, technology transfer, commercialization, education, and training activities;

children.label: B
children.text: a summary of the financial resources allocated to sustainable chemistry initiatives by each participating agency;

children.label: C
children.text: an assessment of the current state of sustainable chemistry in the United States, including the role that Federal agencies are playing in supporting it;

children.label: D
children.text: an analysis of the progress made toward achieving the goals and priorities of this chapter, and recommendations for future program activities;

children.label: E
children.text: an evaluation of steps taken and future strategies to avoid duplication of efforts, streamline interagency coordination, facilitate information sharing, and spread best practices among participating agencies; and

children.label: F
children.text: an evaluation of duplicative Federal funding and duplicative Federal research in sustainable chemistry, efforts undertaken by the Entity to eliminate duplicative funding and research, and recommendations on how to achieve these goals.

children.heading: Submission to GAO
children.label: 2 Submission to GAO
children.text: The Entity shall also submit the report described in paragraph (1) to the Comptroller General of the United States for consideration in future Congressional inquiries.

children.heading: Additional reports
children.label: 3 Additional reports
children.text: The Entity shall submit a report to Congress and the Comptroller General of the United States that incorporates the information described in subparagraphs (A), (B), (D), (E), and (F) of paragraph (1) every 3 years, commencing after the initial report is submitted until the Entity terminates.
